title: fraxinus angustifolia
intro: Fraxinus angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved ash, is a deciduous tree species native to the Mediterranean region. The bark, leaves, and roots of Fraxinus angustifolia have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate various health issues such as pain, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Fraxinus angustifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Fraxinus angustifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
